overview: From 2026-06-15 to 2026-06-16, elizaos/eliza had 16 new PRs (13 merged), 2 new issues, and 8 active contributors.

topIssues.title: Steward login broken in prod: OAuth authorize 400 (code_challenge) + email/send 400 — blocks all Sign in with Eliza Cloud
topIssues.author: NubsCarson
topIssues.body:
## Summary
Steward login is broken in production — **both** email and Google OAuth fail with 400 — which blocks "Sign in with Eliza Cloud" for **every** app (the cli-login flow bounces all third-party apps + the dashboard through this same login).

Reproduces with plain `curl` (no browser, no extensions), so it's a server-side prod issue, not a client environment.

## Symptoms (prod, `api.elizacloud.ai`)
**Google/OAuth** — `GET /steward/auth/oauth/google/authorize?...&response_type=code`:
```
{"ok":false,"error":"code_challenge is required for response_type=code"}
```
Adding `code_challenge` + `code_challenge_method=S256` still returns **400**.

**Email** — `POST /steward/auth/email/send` → **400 Bad Request** (from `steward-login-section.tsx` → `signInWithEmail`).

## Root cause (the OAuth half)
The Steward `/authorize` endpoint now **requires PKCE** (`code_challenge`), but:
- `packages/cloud-frontend/src/pages/login/steward-oauth-url.ts` → `buildStewardOAuthAuthorizeUrl()` builds the URL with `response_type: "code"` and **no** `code_challenge` / `code_challenge_method`.
- The exchange flow is **nonce-based with no verifier**: `packages/cloud-api/auth/steward-nonce-exchange/route.ts` documents *"The Steward API authenticates the exchange purely by possession of the one-time `code` — there is no client [verifier]."*

So the authorize service (PKCE-required) and the cloud login flow (nonce-based, no PKCE) are out of sync — and even a hand-added `code_challenge` still 400s, suggesting a deeper Steward auth misconfig/regression in prod.

## Impact
No one can sign in via Eliza Cloud right now — dashboard, edad, and every third-party app using the cli-login "Sign in with Eliza Cloud" bounce here and dead-end at this login.

## Fix options
- **A (restore parity):** make Steward `/authorize` accept the nonce flow **without** `code_challenge` (matches the existing nonce-exchange contract). Simplest if PKCE was enabled unintentionally.
- **B (full PKCE):** frontend generates a `code_verifier` + S256 `code_challenge`, sends `code_challenge` at `/authorize` and stores the verifier; `steward-nonce-exchange` forwards `code_verifier` to `/auth/oauth/exchange`.

The `email/send` 400 handler isn't in this monorepo (Steward service) — flag for that team too; it may share the same prod regression.

Found via the cli-login flow from a third-party browser app.

topIssues.title: Cloud 10-user launch — tracker
topIssues.author: NubsCarson
topIssues.body:
Single tracker for the cloud 10-user launch. **Core works + verified; the launch is gated on staging-green → prod cutover.** Detailed writeup: #8321 (comment).

## ✅ Done — verified live on prod
- [x] SDK provisions cloud agents (shaw's launch gate) — `/api/v1/eliza/agents` + `/provisioning-agent` → 200
- [x] Monetized money path — elocute + council both earn real $ (live test)
- [x] Profits-pay-hosting loop wired + verified
- [x] #8422 — arm `APPS_DEPLOY_ENABLED` (apps-stuck-`building` root cause) — merged
- [x] #8426 — recommend healthy Cerebras defaults, drop flaky `:nitro` — merged
- [x] #8427 — new orgs default `$0` not `$100` (footgun) — merged

## 🟢 PRs open — review / merge
- [ ] #8430 — wire the apps deploy + tenant-DB E2E into CI (no manual; self-validating)
- [ ] #8433 — edad gets a per-tenant DB → the single all-features test app
- [ ] #8425 — enable `APPS_DEPLOY_ENABLED` on **prod** (DRAFT — merge **only** once staging is green)

## 🛠️ Needs an owner
- [ ] #8428 — release pipeline: grant `NPM_TOKEN` publish access to `@elizaos/*` (lerna E404) — @lalalune
- [ ] bitrouter#572 — `:nitro` 503 (their hosted gateway). Unblocked via Cerebras-direct meanwhile.

## ⏳ Staging → prod cutover — @standujar's lane
- [ ] staging green — headscale up ✅; remaining: cert + `AGENT_TOKEN_PRIVATE_KEY_PEM` + `ELIZA_LOCAL_ROOT_KEY` + `KV_REST_API_URL/TOKEN`
- [ ] terraform prod data plane (`terraform-apps-shared` + `terraform-apps-data-plane`, `environment=production`)
- [ ] merge develop→main (ships #8425's prod flag)
- [ ] arm prod daemon (`arm-apps-daemon.yml -f environment=production` — now includes `APPS_DEPLOY_ENABLED` via #8422)
- [ ] reset stuck app `council` (status=building)
- [ ] deploy edad → loads at `edad.apps.elizacloud.ai` + exercises the whole platform

## Test apps
**edad-chat (#8433)** = the single all-features subject (monetized inference + deploy + per-tenant DB + per-app auth + custom domain). elocute (earns), council (deploy), SupaKan (db) are extras.

cc @standujar @lalalune

topIssues.title: Release pipeline broken: NPM_TOKEN lacks @elizaos/* publish access (lerna E404 on @elizaos/agent)
topIssues.author: NubsCarson
topIssues.body:
## Summary

`release.yaml` has failed on **every** recent run (5/5 on `develop`). `lerna publish` aborts at the first package — `@elizaos/agent` → **`E404 Not found`** — which kills the whole release, so nothing publishes. Result: npm packages are stale (e.g. `@elizaos/cloud-sdk` is `2.0.0-beta.1` on npm vs `2.0.3-beta.13` in the repo).

## Root cause — it's the token, not provenance

`@elizaos/agent` already exists on npm at `0.25.9` (prior owner/era). npm returns **E404** on publish when the authenticated token lacks publish/maintainer rights to an existing scoped package (it masks `403` as `404` to avoid leaking existence). The package itself is fine: `publishConfig.access: "public"`, not private; the workflow already has `id-token: write` + provenance configured.

➡️ **The release `NPM_TOKEN` secret does not have publish access to the `@elizaos` scope** (or to `@elizaos/agent` specifically).

## Evidence

```
lerna verb publish @elizaos/agent
lerna WARN notice Package failed to publish: @elizaos/agent
lerna ERR! E404 Not found
❌ Error: Failed to publish to NPM
```
- npm registry: `@elizaos/agent` → `0.25.9`, `@elizaos/core` → `1.7.2`, `@elizaos/cloud-sdk` → `2.0.0-beta.1`
- repo: all at `2.0.3-beta.13`
- Failing runs: release.yaml on develop, 2026-06-15 (×3) + 2026-06-06 (×2)

## Fix (ops — needs the npm org owner)

@lalalune (or whoever owns the `@elizaos` npm org): grant the automation token behind the `NPM_TOKEN` actions secret **publish/maintainer** access to `@elizaos/*` (whole scope), or rotate `NPM_TOKEN` to one that has it. No code change needed — verified the package.json + workflow permissions are correct.

## Impact / priority

**Not a launch blocker** — the already-published SDK still provisions agents, and #8426 points eliza's default model at Cerebras-direct. But partners installing `@elizaos/cloud-sdk` from npm (e.g. waifu) get the stale `2.0.0-beta.1`. Worth fixing so fresh SDKs ship to integrators.

topPRs.title: ci: make expensive lanes label-gated and explainable
topPRs.author: odilitime
topPRs.body:
## Summary
- add a shared `packages/scripts/ci-path-gate.mjs` classifier for PR path gates
- replace inline bash classifiers in `test.yml` and `scenario-pr.yml` with the shared classifier and richer job summaries
- add lightweight classifier jobs for Docker, mobile, dev smoke, and Windows desktop smoke workflows so expensive jobs can skip with an explanation
- split the long deterministic zero-key E2E jobs into parallel named slices with the existing `Zero-Key Deterministic E2E` checks preserved as aggregate gates
- add force labels for maintainers: `ci:full`, `ci:e2e`, `ci:zero-key`, `ci:server`, `ci:client`, `ci:plugins`, `ci:cloud`, `ci:docker`, `ci:mobile`, `ci:ios`, `ci:android`, `ci:desktop`, `ci:windows`, `ci:dev-smoke`
- document the PR path-gate contract in `.github/workflows/README.md`
- add CI workflow changelog and roadmap docs with the WHYs behind path gates, parallel slices, force labels, and future long-pole work
- self-test the shared classifier in CI before workflow decisions consume it

## Validation
- `bunx @biomejs/biome check packages/scripts/ci-path-gate.mjs packages/scripts/ci-path-gate.self-test.mjs`
- `node --check packages/scripts/ci-path-gate.mjs`
- `node packages/scripts/ci-path-gate.self-test.mjs`
- YAML parse for touched workflow files via Ruby `YAML.load_file`
- `git diff --cached --check`
- `git diff --check`
- staged-tree classifier samples for `test`, `mobile`, and `docker`, including empty labels and `ci:android` label forcing
- YAML parse after splitting the `Tests` and `Scenario PR E2E` deterministic lanes

## Notes
- Push, scheduled, and manual runs keep broad/default behavior.
- PR label events now trigger the gated workflows so maintainers can force lanes without pushing a no-op commit.
- The visible deterministic E2E checks are now aggregate status jobs over parallel slices, so reviewers can inspect the failing surface directly instead of opening one giant serial log.

topPRs.title: fix(auth): Steward OAuth PKCE for all login surfaces (#8257)
topPRs.author: dutchiono
topPRs.body:
## Summary
- Extract PKCE helpers into `@elizaos/shared/steward-session-client` so every Steward OAuth entrypoint sends `code_challenge` at `/authorize` and replays `code_verifier` at exchange.
- **cloud-frontend** login + **os-homepage** checkout OAuth surfaces
- **Feed** `LoginModal` + `/auth/callback/[provider]` via shared module + `POST /api/auth/steward/oauth/exchange` server route

Fixes the OAuth half of #8257 across all monorepo login surfaces.

## Test plan
- [x] `bun run --cwd packages/shared test -- steward-oauth-pkce`
- [ ] GitHub CI after maintainer fork-workflow approval
- [ ] Manual: cloud login, os-homepage checkout, Feed OAuth providers

Fixes #8257 (OAuth half — email/send 400 remains Steward service)

**Note:** Supersedes #8355 (Feed PKCE folded into this PR).

topPRs.title: fix(cloud-api): make container billing idempotent per period (no double-debit on cron re-run)
topPRs.author: 2-A-M
topPRs.body:
## What

Hardens the daily container-billing cron so a re-run (manual trigger, overlapping CF cron, retry) can never double-debit. Defense in depth across three layers, plus a billing reconciliation fix.

**Stacks on #8269** (the free-hosting fix). Until that merges, the diff here includes its single commit; afterward this shrinks to just the four new commits.

## Why

The cron had no idempotency: `convertToCredits` re-debited earnings on every call, `listBillableContainers` ignored the (written-but-never-read) `next_billing_at`, there was no app-level lock (CF cron provides none), and `credit_transactions` recorded `-dailyCost` while `credit_balance` only moved by `-fromCredits` — so the ledger overstated the credit debit by the earnings portion (which is already on the earnings ledger), breaking `credit_balance == sum(credit_transactions)`.

## How

- **Deterministic period.** `computeContainerBillingPeriod(now)` normalizes the run to its UTC day, so the period (and the key derived from it) is stable across same-day re-runs.
- **Earnings idempotency.** `convertToCredits` takes an optional `idempotencyKey` (`container:<id>:<utc-day>`); under the per-user advisory lock it returns the prior `credit_conversion` ledger entry instead of debiting again. Backed by a partial unique index on `redeemable_earnings_ledger((metadata->>'idempotency_key'))`.
- **Query gate.** `listBillableContainers(now)` skips containers whose `next_billing_at` is still in the future (already paid this period).
- **Row-lock guard.** `recordSuccessfulDailyBilling` locks the container row and no-ops if the period is already billed — closing the read→write race between listing and charging. Backed by a partial unique index on `container_billing_records(container_id, billing_period_start) WHERE status='success'`.
- **Reconciliation.** The `credit_transaction` now records `-fromCredits` (the actual credit-balance movement); `dailyCost` and the earnings/credits split stay in metadata.

## Migration (0139)

Adds both partial unique indexes and **dedupes any pre-existing duplicate `success` rows first** (keep earliest by `created_at,id`) so the index can be created on existing prod data — confirmed duplicates can exist today since nothing guarded against them. The ledger index is partial on a non-null key, so existing conversions (which carry no key) are unaffected.

## Verification

- `db:migrate` applies all 140 migrations from scratch incl. 0139 against the real cumulative schema — clean.
- New PGlite-backed suite (`container-billing-idempotency.test.ts`) exercises the real SQL (`FOR UPDATE`, the JSONB-keyed lookup, both partial unique indexes): earnings debit once per key, gate by `next_billing_at`, `-fromCredits` recorded, row-lock no-op on re-run.
- `cloud-shared` + `cloud-api` typecheck clean; biome clean; `db:check-migrations` passes.

topPRs.title: chore: regenerate prompt action docs
topPRs.author: odilitime
topPRs.body:
## Summary
- regenerate \`packages/prompts/specs/actions/plugins.generated.json\`
- regenerate \`packages/core/src/generated/action-docs.ts\`
- bring generated prompt/action docs back in sync with current plugin action sources

## Why
Windows build cascade runs \`@elizaos/prompts:build\`, which rewrites these generated artifacts when they are stale. Keeping them committed avoids generated-output drift failing CI.

## Validation
- \`bun run --cwd packages/prompts build\`
- reran \`bun run --cwd packages/prompts build\` to verify stable output
- \`bunx @biomejs/biome check packages/prompts/specs/actions/plugins.generated.json packages/core/src/generated/action-docs.ts\`
- \`bun run --cwd packages/prompts lint:check\`
- \`git diff --check\`
